svg.font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, 'Helvetica Neue', Arial, sans-serif
svg.aria-label: Memtrace v0.3.29 benchmark — head-to-head vs ChromaDB, GitNexus, and CodeGrapherContext on both find_symbol (exact lookup) and find_code (hybrid retrieval) paths
svg.text: 1,000 queries · 4 systems · same machine, same ground truth · 100% reproducible (MIT) · v0.3.29

g.text: vs ChromaDB 4.5 min · Mem0 / Graphiti 1–2 hrs · $0 LLM
runs lighter than a Chrome tab
Head-to-Head — what you actually do all day
A real codebase (Django · ~3,300 files · 50,000 symbols · 22× larger)
Describe intent, not a name (find_code · NEW v0.3.29) 3,000 cases · exact + split + typo
Find by intent on a real codebase (Django)
Find with typo or split words (mempalace)
The bill at the end of the month
LLM API cost to index a repo
Bottom line: hybrid retrieval is more accurate than every published competitor —
exact lookup is still sub-millisecond. Local, $0 LLM, 26 MB. Memtrace leads by 1.4× to 778×.
the parts the others can't even attempt
"show me this codebase 3 weeks ago" — every symbol versioned
6 modes: impact · novelty · recency · directional · compound · overview
"what breaks if I change this?" — full transitive callers in one call
per-symbol scoring, AST-derived — flag the hard-to-reason functions
"these symbols always move together" — surface hidden coupling
normalized body hash — find the 4 places that re-implement the same thing
frontend call → backend endpoint, automatically wired across repos
find_central_symbols · find_bridge_symbols — what holds the codebase up
native Levenshtein — agent typos still return the right symbol
named execution pipelines through the call graph — debug the journey
automatic subsystem boundaries — what's tightly coupled, what isn't
every save creates an episode — your in-progress code is in the graph
the implementation with 47 callers comes back ahead of the test fixture
Vector search retrieves snippets. Code graphers list filenames.
Memtrace gives the agent a memory — every symbol, every change, every connection, ranked.
Try it: npm install -g memtrace → memtrace start
Auto-indexes your project · MCP-ready for Claude Code & Cursor v2.4+ · MIT-licensed · runs offline
